title: Repealing Big Brother Overreach Act
plain_summary: This bill would get rid of a law called the Corporate Transparency Act that was passed in 2021. That law requires companies to report information about who really owns them to the government. The government uses this information to help stop terrorism funding and money laundering. If this new bill passes, companies would no longer have to give the government this ownership information. The bill would also clean up other laws by removing mentions of the canceled act.
last_action_text: Ordered to be Reported by the Yeas and Nays: 26 - 25.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This Act can be called the Repealing Big Brother Overreach Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Repeal
sections.text: This section cancels a law called the Corporate Transparency Act. The Corporate Transparency Act was part of the William M. Mac Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year two thousand and twenty-one. This section also cancels all the changes that the Corporate Transparency Act made to other laws. The section then makes small changes to fix problems in the legal code that will happen when the Corporate Transparency Act is removed. These changes remove references to section five thousand three hundred and thirty-six from several places in Title thirty-one of the United States Code. The section also removes or changes parts of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of two thousand and twenty that were connected to the Corporate Transparency Act.
